preconditions: All source and destination GRs are legal and initialized
expected_results: Every implemented opcode produces the independent per-lane golden
allowed_results: The exact 64-bit result declared for each of 54 opcodes
permitted_state_changes: Selected destination GR and its NaT bit
required_unchanged_state: Source GRs, unrelated registers, predicates, and memory
cases.committed_prefix: Each enabled packed operation commits one complete destination value
cases.restart_action: Not exercised for legal mapped operations

oracle.derivation: Test-owned lane extraction, insertion, sign extension, and result composition do not import production decode or translation tables

preconditions: Lane inputs straddle positive and negative saturation limits
inputs: Modulo, signed-saturating, unsigned-saturating, shift-add, and pack operands
expected_results: Each lane clamps or wraps at the exact selected boundary
allowed_results: Independent signed, unsigned, and modulo lane goldens
cases.committed_prefix: All lane results commit atomically as one GR write

oracle.derivation: Python integer bounds and explicit lane masks define saturation without using translator helpers

preconditions: Lane pairs contain equal, greater, and lesser values
inputs: pcmp equality and signed greater-than at every implemented width
expected_results: True lanes contain all ones and false lanes contain zero
cases.committed_prefix: The composed comparison mask commits as one destination write

oracle.derivation: Test-owned signed lane conversion and Python comparisons define each mask independently

preconditions: Every input lane has a distinguishable value
inputs: Average, min/max, multiply, SAD, mix, mux, pack, unpack, and zero-scan forms
expected_results: Lane selection, ordering, rounding, widening, narrowing, and reduction match literal goldens
cases.committed_prefix: Each arrangement or reduction commits one destination value

oracle.derivation: Explicit Python lane permutations and arithmetic reductions define results without production lookup tables

preconditions: Signed and unsigned lanes expose shifted-in bits
inputs: Fixed and variable counts below, at, and beyond lane width
expected_results: Logical and arithmetic shifts use the exact architected boundary result per lane
allowed_results: Independent fixed and variable shift goldens
required_unchanged_state: Shift count source, value source, and unrelated state
cases.committed_prefix: All shifted lanes commit in one GR write

oracle.derivation: Test-owned count clamping, lane masks, and signed shifts define boundary results

preconditions: Predicate, alias, NaT, reserved MUX1, and legal A-unit slot fixtures are explicit
inputs: True and false qp, same-group GR alias, real ld8.fill NaT, reserved immediate, and A9 in M/I slots
expected_results: Predication suppresses effects, aliases read group-entry state, NaT propagates, legal slots execute, and enabled reserved fields fault precisely
allowed_results: Exact destination/NaT images or exact Illegal Operation state
permitted_state_changes: Enabled legal destination or architectural interruption state
fault_priority: A false qualifying predicate suppresses the reserved-field fault; an enabled reserved encoding faults before target or suffix effects
cases.committed_prefix: Legal packed writes commit once; enabled reserved fields preserve the prefix and suppress faulting/suffix effects
cases.restart_action: The enabled reserved encoding reports its exact bundle and slot for restart

oracle.derivation: Literal encodings, group-entry values, NaT setup, vector addresses, and fault-slot expectations are owned by the test
